escalations.reason: Spec/plan self-contradiction on audit-log completeness (enterprise-arch concern), surfaced by the test-designer while writing TEST-218-223/259/271-279. REQ-013's acceptance clause literally requires an audit line for 'a rejected (unknown-sender / root-not-allowed) ... action' -- i.e. BOTH rejection kinds get audited. But 03-plan.md TASK-010 explicitly designs the opposite for the unknown-sender case: 'The unknown-sender path returns WITHOUT ever calling into the dispatcher (so no audit-log entry is written for it either -- REQ-003's without invoking any CLI is satisfied trivially since sender-rejection happens strictly before the dispatcher method runs).' These cannot both be true as written: TASK-010's registrar-level sender check happens BEFORE the dispatcher (and its injected OrkyActionAuditLog) is ever reached, so an unknown-sender rejection structurally cannot produce an audit line under the current file/module boundary (register-orky-action.ts has no audit-log reference; only orky-action-dispatcher.ts does). The test-designer did not adjudicate this: it pinned tests to the TASK-010-consistent behavior (register-orky-action.test.ts TEST-272 asserts the dispatcher is never invoked on unknown-sender) and satisfied REQ-013's acceptance criterion using only the root-not-allowed case (an unambiguous case that does reach the dispatcher), leaving the unknown-sender-audit question genuinely open. This is exactly the kind of design gap the coordinator was told to escalate rather than guess on, since it is the FIRST write-capable, project-mutating IPC surface in the app and enterprise-arch/audit-completeness was called out as a first-class concern in both 01-concept.md and 02-spec.md. Two resolution paths exist, both mechanical but requiring a REAL decision before implement: (A) accept TASK-010 as-is and narrow REQ-013's acceptance text to 'every action that reaches the dispatcher is audited; a sender rejected at the registrar boundary is not' (spec edit, minor, keeps the current clean separation where register-orky-action.ts never imports the audit log); or (B) thread an OrkyActionAuditLog reference into register-orky-action.ts so a registrar-level unknown-sender rejection is ALSO audited (plan edit to TASK-010, adds a dependency from the registrar to a TASK-005 artifact it currently does not use, and needs a decision on whether the registrar or the dispatcher owns writing that specific line). My recommendation: (A) is more consistent with REQ-003's own acceptance text ('a rejected sender ... returns ... WITHOUT ... invoking any CLI' -- silent on audit) and keeps the security-relevant boundary crisp (sender validation is a hard gate BEFORE any project-scoped action is even attempted, so there is arguably nothing project-specific yet to attribute), but a security-minded read could reasonably want EVERY dispatch attempt against this write-capable surface logged, including rejected ones, for after-the-fact forensics of an unrecognized/rogue sender probing the surface -- which is closer to a literal reading of REQ-013's own words and enterprise-arch's stated intent. I am not deciding this myself; recommend the human choose (A) or (B) before the implement phase, since it determines TASK-010's actual file boundary and whether register-orky-action.test.ts's TEST-272 needs a follow-up test-designer amendment.
escalations.decision: Approved (A): narrow REQ-013's acceptance text to 'every action that reaches the dispatcher is audited; a sender rejected at the registrar boundary (unknown-sender) is not audited.' Keeps the clean module boundary (registrar never imports the audit log). No plan/TASK-010 change needed; the tests already pinned to this behavior are correct as-is -- update 02-spec.md's REQ-013 wording to match reality, loop back to spec only far enough to fix the wording, then re-flow through plan/tests (should be a no-op re-verify since TASK-010/04-tests.md already match this reading) and on to implement.

escalations.reason: Implement gate FAILS (npm test exits 1) for reasons that are NOT implementation bugs -- retrying the implement phase (Gatekeeper's own drive() suggests retry-phase, attempt 2/3) cannot fix either failure, since both are pre-existing defects in FROZEN test content the implementer correctly declined to touch. (1) TEST-156 (tests/shared/orky-action-validate.test.ts, 0007's OWN frozen suite) asserts all 6 messages from validateFeatureSlug(['a/b','a\b',...]) are pairwise-unique (new Set(msgs).size === msgs.length), but TEST-150/TEST-151 in the SAME frozen file explicitly pin 'a/b' and 'a\b' to the SAME single-segment-violation message by design (03-plan.md TASK-002: one shared message for any path-separator violation) -- so TEST-156 is mathematically unsatisfiable by ANY implementation; it contradicts its own sibling tests. (2) TEST-031 (tests/main/orky-osc-structural.test.ts) is a PRE-EXISTING frozen test from an already-shipped, already-merged feature (0014-orky-osc-heartbeat) asserting CH contains EXACTLY the 3 pre-0014 orky-prefixed identifiers via a broad /^orky[A-Z]/ identifier-name regex -- but 0007's OWN frozen spec (REQ-018) mandates adding exactly 4 new orkyAction:* channels (orkyActionResolveEscalation/submitWork/recordHumanGate/driveStatus), all of which match that same identifier regex even though their wire VALUES (orkyAction:*) do not collide with the read-only orky: domain (TEST-031's own SECOND, value-prefix-based assertion already passes fine -- only the redundant identifier-name heuristic breaks). This is the identifier-namespace analogue of CONV-012 (a frozen structural test pinning an invariant on a namespace multiple features legitimately extend) but the affected file belongs to a DIFFERENT, already-completed feature, not 0007 -- editing it retroactively touches 0014's frozen historical gate record, which is outside what my coordinator briefed me to decide unilaterally. My recommendation: loop back to tests, dispatch a fresh test-designer to (a) relax TEST-156 to accept the intentional shared a/b-vs-a-backslash-b message (assert the remaining messages are unique plus that these two specific ones match each other, or reduce the uniqueness set to exclude that pair), and (b) narrow TEST-031's first assertion to check only the orky: wire-value prefix (the invariant it actually protects, and which its own second assertion already checks) rather than the broader orky-identifier-name heuristic -- then, per doc-sync, propose a CONV entry generalizing CONV-012 to shared identifier-namespace structural tests, not just shared source files. I am not editing either test myself pending this decision.

loopbacks.reason: ESC-001 resolved (A): narrow REQ-013's acceptance text to 'every action that reaches the dispatcher is audited; a sender rejected at the registrar boundary (unknown-sender) is not.' Wording-only spec fix -- TASK-010/04-tests.md already match this reading, no plan/test content change expected, just re-earning the frozen gates after the spec-freeze-invalidating edit.
